title: cota halophila
intro: Cota halophila, also known as sea bindweed, is a species of flowering plant in the dandelion tribe within the sunflower family. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. The plant is said to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, soothing digestive issues, and helping to manage respiratory problems. Cota halophila is commonly used in herbal preparations, such as teas and infusions, to harness its medicinal properties and provide relief from various health issues.
